title: Central Bureau of Statistics (Syria)

infobox.value: This article about [government](/wiki/Politics_of_Syria "Politics of Syria") in [Syria](/wiki/Syria "Syria") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Central_Bureau_of_Statistics_(Syria)&action=edit).

(**CBS**) ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): المكتب المركزي للإحصاء) is the [statistical](/wiki/Statistical "Statistical") agency responsible for the gathering of "information relating to economic, social and general activities and conditions" in the [Syrian Arab Republic](/wiki/Syrian_Arab_Republic "Syrian Arab Republic"). The office is answerable to the office of the [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Syria "Prime Minister of Syria") and has its main offices in [Damascus](/wiki/Damascus "Damascus"). The CBS was established in 2005 and is administered by an administrative council headed by the deputy prime minister for economic affairs.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

["Archived copy"](https://web.archive.org/web/20151208114457/http://www.cbssyr.sy/Introduction.htm). Archived from [the original](http://www.cbssyr.sy/Introduction.htm) on 2015-12-08. Retrieved 2015-10-18.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: archived copy as title ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_archived_copy_as_title "Category:CS1 maint: archived copy as title"))

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Syrian government institution in charge of statistics and census data

The **Central Bureau of Statistics** (**CBS**) ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): المكتب المركزي للإحصاء) is the [statistical](/wiki/Statistical "Statistical") agency responsible for the gathering of "information relating to economic, social and general activities and conditions" in the [Syrian Arab Republic](/wiki/Syrian_Arab_Republic "Syrian Arab Republic"). The office is answerable to the office of the [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Syria "Prime Minister of Syria") and has its main offices in [Damascus](/wiki/Damascus "Damascus"). The CBS was established in 2005 and is administered by an administrative council headed by the deputy prime minister for economic affairs.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

After the Syrian government began reconstructing infrastructure in 2011, the bureau began releasing data from 2011 to 2018.
